description:
You are a platform engineer who needs to add a lightweight backup step to a
    CI/CD runner’s workspace.

    1. The workspace already contains a directory  
       /home/user/ci_cd/logs/  
       holding three plain-text log files that are produced after every pipeline
       run:
         • stage1.log  
         • stage2.log  
         • stage3.log  

    2. Create a compressed tar archive that contains **only** the three log
       files listed above.  
       • Name the archive   ci_logs_backup.tar.gz  
       • Save it inside a newly-created directory  
         /home/user/backups/  

    3. After the archive is created, generate one human-readable report file
       called  backup_report.txt  in the same /home/user/backups/ directory.
       The report must consist of exactly **three** newline-terminated lines in
       the following fixed, testable format (do not add extra spaces):

           ARCHIVE: /home/user/backups/ci_logs_backup.tar.gz
           FILECOUNT: 3
           STATUS: SUCCESS

       Notes on the format:
       • All three lines must appear in the order shown.  
       • “ARCHIVE:” must be followed by the full absolute path to the archive you
         just created.  
       • “FILECOUNT:” must be the decimal number 3 (no punctuation).  
       • “STATUS:” must literally be the word SUCCESS (all capitals).  
       • Each line must end with a single newline character (no carriage
         returns, no blank lines before or after).

    4. You do **not** need to list file sizes, timestamps or anything else.
       Writing the three lines above is sufficient.

    Deliverables that the automated tests will check:
       • The archive file /home/user/backups/ci_logs_backup.tar.gz exists and,
         when extracted, yields stage1.log, stage2.log and stage3.log in its
         root.  
       • The report file /home/user/backups/backup_report.txt exists and its
         content exactly matches the three-line template shown above.

    A typical solution should take only about three or four standard shell
    commands. Feel free to verify your work with additional commands (e.g.,
    listing directories), but only the presence and exact contents of the two
    deliverable files will be graded.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the task starts:

    Directory: /home/user/ci_cd/logs/
        stage1.log      (content: "Stage 1 completed\n")
        stage2.log      (content: "Stage 2 completed\n")
        stage3.log      (content: "Stage 3 completed\n")

    Nothing else exists inside /home/user/backups/ prior to task start.

    Expected artifacts after correct task completion:

    1. /home/user/backups/ci_logs_backup.tar.gz  
       • A gzip-compressed tar archive.  
       • When extracted (tar -xzf), it produces exactly three entries at the
         archive root:
             stage1.log
             stage2.log
             stage3.log
       • No sub-directories or extra files are present in the archive.  
       • Each extracted file’s content matches the originals listed above.

    2. /home/user/backups/backup_report.txt  
       Exact byte-for-byte content (line-feeds = 0x0A):

           ARCHIVE: /home/user/backups/ci_logs_backup.tar.gz\n
           FILECOUNT: 3\n
           STATUS: SUCCESS\n
name: task_000000_4c9883f6
